opps.title: INTEGRATED PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SERVICES
opps.description:
The Coast Guard Accessions Service Center (CG ASC) seeks contractor support to enhance recruiting operations through program management, analytical services, and advanced information technology. A key focus of this requirement is the development, deployment, and sustainment of custom business applications using Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI). The contractor will deliver business process automation solutions, including Robotic Process Automation (RPA), to streamline operations, improve efficiency, and support data-driven decision-making.

Additionally, the contractor will provide graphic design and content creation services to support both internal and external communications. This includes designing user interfaces for applications, developing marketing materials, and producing visually engaging content for outreach and stakeholder engagement. All content must align with Coast Guard branding and approval protocols.

Other services include planning and analysis led by Project Management Professional (PMP) certified personnel, performance management, strategic planning, meeting facilitation, documentation, process improvement, change coordination, reporting, and knowledge management. Personnel must have relevant certifications and government experience.

The period of performance is one base year plus four option years. Work will primarily occur at Coast Guard Headquarters in Washington DC, with possible off-site work as approved. All contractor staff must meet pre-employment screening and background investigation requirements.

opps.title: Inventory Management System Public  Housing Information Center Support
opps.agency: Department of Housing and Urban Development
opps.description:
The Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), Office of Public and Indian Housing (PIH), Real Estate Assessment Center (REAC), requires contractor support to provide comprehensive data analytics, reporting, dashboard development, and business intelligence services for the PIH portfolio. This requirement will be awarded as a Fixed-Price Fixed Unit Rate (FP-FUR) MAS Task Order and is a Total Small Business Set-Aside. See the attached solicitation for complete requirements, instructions, and evaluation criteria.

This request for quote is issued on conditional approval. Final approval and funding is being processed. HUD will not pay for quote preparation costs, if final approval and funding is not obtained to award this order.

This RFQ is for FP-FUR and NOT for FFP.

opps.title: Continuous Monitoring & Agile Security Services (CMASS) IV
opps.description:
***03/05/2026: Estimated solicitation release date updated to 03/31/2026, estimated period of performance start date updated to 09/16/2026, and anticipated award date updated to 09/01/2026.***
***01/13/2025: Estimated solicitation release date updated to 03/02/2026.***
***10/29/2025: Estimated solicitation release date updated from 11/03/2025 to 01/13/2026; Small business se aside updated from N/A to Full; Small business program updated from SB to 8(a); Anticipated award updated from 3/1/26 to 4/1/26***
***9/24/2025: Estimated solicitation release date updated from 09/11/2025 to 11/03/2025; Dollar Range updated from $10M-$20M to $5M-$10M.***

USCIS has a requirement for Continuous Monitoring & Agile Security Services (CMASS).  USCIS seeks to establish a more sustainable and effective continuous monitoring program, leveraging data from security and governance tools to enhance our ability to identify and mitigate cybersecurity risks, evaluate emerging technologies, streamline security-related tasks. Additionally, we seek to transform the Ongoing Authorization (OA) program to incorporate new capabilities and risk models for Machine Learning (ML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) and implement appropriate governance measures. The CMASS resources will continue to support security authorization and continuous monitoring activities, the Vulnerability Management & Configuration Management Programs, role-based cybersecurity training, and system stakeholder outreach to ensure USCIS meets security and DHS Information System Performance Plan (ISPP) requirements.
win_reasons: Partial comms scope match — verify fit vs CMS/CDC/HUD delivery experience

opps.title: Immigration Biometrics and Identity Services (IBIS)
opps.description: The combined USCIS Immigration Biometrics and Identity Services (IBIS) project will deliver secure, scalable, and modern IT solutions supporting data analytics, machine learning, entity resolution, biometrics and identity management, and scheduling for the nationâ€™s immigration system. Leveraging agile methodologies and advanced Development, Security, and Operations (DevSecOps) practices, multi-disciplinary teams develop, operate, and maintain cloud-based applications using a robust suite of open-source and commercial tools. The project emphasizes automation, continuous integration and delivery, and strict adherence to federal security, privacy, and accessibility standards. Teams are responsible for end-to-end development, operations, security, and technical support, ensuring high-quality code, rapid incident response, and comprehensive documentation. This integrated approach enables USCIS to efficiently and securely manage complex identity and biometrics systems for both internal and public-facing applications.

opps.title: Request for Information (RFI) School Choice Education Voucher Platform
opps.description:
This request for information (RFI) is issued solely for information and planning and does not constitute a request for quote (RFQ) or a promise to issue an RFQ in the future.

The Department of War Education Activity (DoWEA) seeks information on how an interested contractor could provide a commercially available, proven platform to administer proposed school choice education voucher program. This program is expected to support approximately 11,000 dependents of military personnel located at US bases/installations.

DoWEA anticipates making one award for a one base year and four option years. The anticipated NAICS code for this requirement is 518210 (Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services).  Note: Release of a formal solicitation is contingent on the enactment of pending legislative authority and appropriation of funds.   

Please see attached Request for Information (RFI) for more details.

opps.title: Visual Arts Support Services.
opps.description:
The U.S. Fire Administration (USFA), located at 16825 S. Seton Avenue, Emmitsburg, Maryland, requires visual arts support to provide professional graphic design services for USFA and NDEMU staff. The graphic and multimedia design staff will be located onsite at USFA.

Design staff will create visual assets that highlight and elevate USFAâ€™s brand, reinforcing its position as a trusted leader in fire and emergency medical services. Visual arts support services are expected to recommend best design practices.

The government will provide all necessary graphic design software and material supplies.

Design staff responsibilities include:

Publication layout and digital formatting
Training materials
Factsheets, handouts, flyers, and infographics
Illustrations
3D and 2D animations and simulations
Animated and motion graphics video productions
Program graphics and posters
Production work (large format printing, laminating, etc.)
Limited studio photography

opps.title: One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus (OASIS+) 8(a) Small Business Set-Aside Continuously Open Solicitation
opps.description:
May 12, 2026 The U.S. General Services Administration’s Office of Acquisition Solutions Development (ASD) is pleased to announce the first Phase II apparent awardee notifications for OASIS+ 8(a) Small Business Set-Aside. A consolidated list of Phase II apparent awardees for all OASIS+ contract families may be accessed on the Solicitations (continuously open) page (see “Links” section within this SAM.gov posting). This list will be updated with additional apparent awardees as award announcements continue to be made.

Award notifications have been sent via email to offerors who have been determined successful in one or more proposed domains. Apparent awardees are reminded to ensure that their SAM.gov entity information is active, accurate, and complete at all times. The program plans to issue formal awards and notices to proceed (NTPs) to qualified contractors on or about May 22, 2026 after final legal reviews occur. The newly awarded contracts will be available for ordering in GSA eBuy one business day following the issuance of formal awards and NTPs and upon contractor registration within the Vendor Support Center (VSC), whichever is later. Contract awardees must register their contracts in VSC before they will be visible in eBuy and available for procurement. 

Please note that GSA continues to release answers to RFP questions deemed broadly beneficial and relevant on a regular basis on the program website Solicitations (continuously open) page. Questions related to the award announcements should be submitted via email to the OASIS+ Contracting Officers at oasisplusmods@gsa.gov.

Please continue to monitor this OASIS+ Interact Community forum where you can join and subscribe to get notifications about announcements and program updates. For more information about the OASIS+ Program, visit: The OASIS+ website. Also, follow our social media channels X and LinkedIn. 

************************************************************************************

opps.title: Decennial Transformation & Application Modernization Acquisition
opps.description: The CenTAM Acquisition Framework supports the Business Ecosystem, the Decennial (2030 Census and early 2040 Census research), as well as IT, economic, geographic, demographic, research methodology programs, and other program areas within USCB. The purpose is to plan, develop, document, test, train, monitor, manage, and maintain software architecture and applications to support the 2030 Decennial Census operations. DTAM is the modernization of existing applications and the development of new applications, across Decennial systems, that will be leveraged for the Decennial Census Programs, in support of the 2028 Census Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Decennial Census.
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)
Partial cloud/modernization match — Clarity24 JV has NOAA cloud delivery experience
Small business set-aside

pp_refs: NOAA NWS IDP Cloud Transition / App Mod (Clarity24, $7M)
tasks.note: BLN24 JV — Clarity24 NOAA app mod $2.8M, Census MAF/TIGER modernization

capability_matrix.fit_reason: Can prime most: Application Modernization. Some areas need partner

qualifying.q: Do we have the capabilities needed?

qualifying.answer: Forecast — early awareness, shape before RFP
$100.0M — Tier 2+ (BLN24 has won at this range)

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Census Bureau's massive digital modernization initiative — the CenTAM framework — covering IT systems for the 2030 Decennial Census. Includes application development, legacy modernization, cloud migration, data collection platforms, and systems to support economic, geographic, and demographic programs. Multiple separate contract awards expected under this framework.
rex_analysis.rex_take: Clarity24 JV has direct Census past performance ($4.5M MAF/TIGER modernization, $4.5M EDL Applications, $2.8M NOAA app mod). Census is also BLN24's highest-value client ($18.4M). This is a real opportunity — but it's a large technology modernization vehicle, not comms or HCD. The question is which specific task areas under CenTAM align with what Clarity24 can actually deliver vs. what larger primes will own. Full & Open means big integrators will compete.
rex_analysis.watch_notes: Get into the weeds on the CenTAM acquisition structure — it's a collection of complementary contracts, not one big award. Find which specific package maps to digital modernization vs. IT infrastructure vs. data engineering. Clarity24 should be positioned for the digital/app mod pieces. Due 2027 — shape now.

opps.title: Cyber Grant Support and Program Development
opps.description:
Summary of SOW Requirements: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS), via the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) Integrated Operations Division (IOD), requires the Contractor to deliver comprehensive support for the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program (SLCGP) and Tribal Cybersecurity Grant Program (TCGP). The Contractor will actively advance program development, maintain information continuity, and ensure operational excellence across CISA regions, fostering strong coordination with CISA Divisions, Mission Enabling Offices, and interagency partners.

Scope and Objectives: The Contractor is responsible for both administrative and programmatic support to IOD, facilitating communication, capacity building, and awareness of cybersecurity grant programs. The Contractor will coordinate efforts across CISA regions and headquarters, strengthen regional capabilities, support service delivery, and execute IOD priorities.

Key Tasks and Requirements:
1. Program Support and Development: Collect and analyze best practices, lessons learned, and requirements to drive continuous improvement and inform future grant opportunities. Provide relevant documents and facilitate delivery of grant programs across regions, enhancing cyber competency among security advisors, analysts, and outreach staff. Deliver training to regional staff, ensuring readiness and effective execution. Establish mechanisms to capture return on investment (ROI) through program monitoring, and manage technical assistance for Cybersecurity Advisors and State Coordinators. Support annual program monitoring and grant close-out activities.

2. Operational and Strategic Engagement: Maintain and enhance tools such as the Service Change Request Tool and IOD Intake Process to gather requirements, prioritize services, and transition to full operating capability. Improve dashboards and automate reporting, including integration with ServiceNow. Develop engagement plans and public-facing material
win_reasons: Partial comms scope match — verify fit vs CMS/CDC/HUD delivery experience
8(a) set-aside — direct vehicle match for BLN24
⚠️ Gap area detected: Cybersecurity, Training & eLearning, CRM / Salesforce — would need a teaming partner for this scope

Dept of Education EDARMST Data Analytics ($2.9M)

pp_refs: HHS OIDP Comprehensive Communications ($10.3M, Fors Marsh JV)
tasks.note: BLN24 prime — HUD FHEO, HRSA, USCIS outreach work

BLN24 gap — no CRM implementation prime on record

opps.summary:

opps.title: Management and Governance Directorate (MGD) Financial Analytics, Governance, and Acquisition Support
opps.description:
This re-compete acquisition seeks comprehensive professional support services for the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Office of Information Technology (OIT)'s Management and Governance Directorate (MGD), Financial Management Acquisition Division (FMAD), and Strategic Planning & Mission Engagement Division (SPME). Services encompass strategy implementation, financial planning, and investment management, aiming to enhance OIT's budget formulation, planning, and reporting, and ensure transparent, data-driven financial decision-making. Key support areas include Capital Planning and Investment Control (CPIC) coordination, strategic financial planning, strategy management, and critical support for the Unified Immigration Portal (UIP) program, focusing on cost models and reporting. The requirement also involves extensive analytics, automation, data visualization, and platforms technical services, including the development and maintenance of executive dashboards (e.g., Power BI, Power Platform, Apptio Cloud ability). These services are essential for managing extensive financial resources and delivering mission-critical IT capabilities that support trade administration, traveler movement, and border security.

 Acquisition Strategy: 100% Small Business Set-Aside competed under General Services Administration (GSA) OASIS+ Small Business (SB) Pool.

opps.title: Program Management Office Support
opps.description:
This requirement is for contractor support to provide three Agile-driven teams to deliver program management, risk management, and knowledge management services for the Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP) systems and programs. The contractor will support performance measurement, process improvement, data analytics, requirements management, and business case development for SEVP operations. Responsibilities include developing and maintaining risk management plans for the Student and Exchange Visitor Information System (SEVIS) Programs Section, SEVP Business Automation Section, and System Management Unit. The contractor support team will coordinate risk reviews, provide independent risk assessments, and manage risk inventories and mitigation strategies.

Additionally, the contractor team will support the operational management of program documentation and knowledge libraries within the SEVP Assessment Management System (SEVPAMS), ensuring proper version control and metadata management. Strategic planning and administrative support will be provided to facilitate efficient operations, compliance with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) privacy and information assurance policies, and continuous improvement of SEVP systems and processes. The scope excludes software development and focuses on management, administration, and support functions.

opps.title: Software Development, Operations, Maintenance (O&M) Support Services
opps.description:
The overall scope of this BPA statement of work (SOW) covers Enterprise Architecture, Systems/Applications/Platforms Architecture, Systems/Applications/Platforms Development/Modernization and Enhancements (DME), Operations and Maintenance (O&M), DevOps, and Customer Support Center services for DAPS. Specifically, the services shall cover all phases of the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC), including gathering and analysis, Architecture, modernization, Proof of Concepts (POC)/Pilots, solution design and development, unit and functional testing, integration testing, DevOps, integration, migration, customer support center services, and documentation. 

In alignment with Departmental/HHS priorities, this effort is intended to support not only HRSA's current organizational structure and mission needs, but also evolving HHS governance models, enterprise initiatives, and future operating constructs such as the Administration for a Healthy America (AHA), a planned operating agency within the HHS. The Department periodically undertakes organizational realignments, consolidations, and the establishment of new operating or staff divisions to improve efficiency, coordination, and service delivery across public health programs. 

Accordingly, the scope of services contemplated under this contract is designed to remain applicable and adaptable to future HHS-wide transformations, including but not limited to the transfer, consolidation, integration, or realignment of HRSA functions, programs, systems, or responsibilities into newly established or successor organizations, operating divisions, or centralized HHS components. This includes support for any future Departmental entities, shared services, or governance structures that assume responsibility for programs, systems, or missions currently performed by HRSA and its merging components from an IT perspective. In the event the order has cost growth as a result of the government buying more of same within scope due to HHS restr

opps.title: Updated: Enterprise Remote Document Authentication
opps.description:
*** 5/19/2026: USCIS anticipates releasing a solicitation on NASA SEWP V set-aside to small business and anticipates solicitation release in early June. This requirement will be competed without any brand-name specifications.***

USCIS anticipate a requirement for a commercial software solution for document authentication and identity verification service. These services are essential forÂ maintainingÂ program integrity, supporting federal operations, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. Historically, many USCIS processes have relied on manual, in-person document review, which can present operational challenges and limit access and flexibility,Â especially for remote or distributed users. More specifically, the solution will capture high quality images of identity and work authorization documents, apply Presentation Attack Detection (PAD) and face match checks, use template and barcode/MRZ based authentication, and extract structured data via Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for use in USCIS-controlled microservices. All processing will occur within USCIS or approved Department of Homeland Security (DHS) environments, in compliance with DHS security and privacy requirements.

opps.title: Microsoft 365 Management, Operational Support, and Enhancements (MOSE)
opps.description:
USCIS has a requirement for Microsoft 365 Management, Operational Support, and Enhancements (MOSE). This Operations and Maintenance Services requirement will assist USCIS to operate, optimize, enhance and maintain existing M365 services, onboard new M365 services, and plan and develop strategies for future service sustainment, not limited to, but including: Core productivity applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, OneNote); Collaboration and Communication (Microsoft Team, SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business, Loop, Whiteboard); Security and Compliance (Microsoft Purview, Defender, Entra ID, Intune, Rights Management and Information protection); Power Platform (Power Bi, Apps, Automate and Virtual Agents); Project and Task Management (Planner, To Do, List); Additional Services and Tools (Booking, Forms, Stream, Sway); Artificial Intelligence and Automation (Microsoft Copilot).  Assisting in planning and executing the rollout of Copilot /AI service in accordance with DHS and USCIS security and privacy policies as well as additional Microsoft 365 Services, USCIS may choose to adopt.   Additionally, USCIS needs support for M365 service integrations with both Microsoft and non-Microsoft Technologies, e.g., AirWatch, ServiceNow, Archive360, JAMF, and AgilQuest.

*Small business set aside to be determined through market research. USCIS intends to issue a Request for Information during May 2026.

opps.title: MRI Annotation for Computer Vision analyses
opps.agency: NIH Office of Logistics and Acquisition Operations
opps.description:
his project utilizes high-resolution Whole-Body MRI to support the Natural History study, providing specialized diagnostic interpretation for a 200-case cohort. 

                                                 Requirements



Scope of Work 

We are developing a computer vision pipeline to detect and quantify inflammation in WBMRI scans. We need structured image annotations to train and validate deep learning models. 



TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS 

Independently and not as an agent of the Government, the Contractor shall be required to furnish all the necessary services, qualified personnel, material, equipment, and facilities, not otherwise provided by the Government, as needed to perform this Statement of Work (SOW). 



Work will be issued via request from the Contracting Officer (CO). Each request will provide the specific details and requirements necessary for completion of the work. The Contracting Officer Representative (COR) and the Program Task Leader (TL) will determine the tasks with the CO. 




	MANAGEMENT AND ADMINISTRATION 




The Contractor shall: 


	Provide overall project management and communications, tracking, monitoring, and reporting on project status and progress.  



	Utilize provided RedCap survey to record data. 



	Provide integration and coordination of all contract activities. 



	Recommend modifications to project requirements and timelines. 



	Notify the COR and CO of any problems encountered in completing the work within a request. 



	Provide status updates at a minimum of monthly intervals to review the status of approved projects and to discuss any matters relevant to the scientific (with COR and TL) and financial administration (with COR and CO) of the contract and future activities. 





	TASKS 




Data Description 


	Population: Study participants with Spondyloarthritis 



	Modality: Whole-body MRI 



	Sequences for review: 



	Coronal STIR Whole Body 



	Oblique Coronal STIR Sacrum 



	Axial STIR Whole Pelvis
opps.summary:
Background
 This project is initiated to utilize high-resolution Whole-Body MRI (WBMRI) to support a Natural History study, which aims to provide specialized diagnostic interpretation for a cohort of 200 cases. 
The goal is to develop a computer vision pipeline that can detect and quantify inflammation in WBMRI scans.

Work Details
 The Contractor shall independently furnish all necessary services, qualified personnel, materials, equipment, and facilities required to perform the Statement of Work (SOW). 
The work will be issued via requests from the Contracting Officer (CO), with specific deta

opps.description:
Amendment 0002, June 10, 2026

The due date for proposals has been extended to 4:00 PM Eastern Time, on June 17, 2026.  The project team is still working on answers to questions received.  A subsequent amendment will be published containing answers to questions received. 

Amendment 0001, June 3 2026

The due date for proposals has been extended to 4:00 PM Eastern Time, on June 10, 2026. 

Description 

This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products or commercial services prepared in accordance with FAR parts 12 and 15. This combined synopsis constitutes the only solicitation. Solicitation number 47QACA26R0274 is issued as a request for proposals (RFP) for a Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) for  loan file review services. 

This acquisition is set-aside for small business concerns. This solicitation incorporates provisions and clauses by reference. The full text of provisions and clauses may be accessed electronically at www.acquisition.gov. 

In accordance with FAR 12.202(b)(1) the following information is being included in this synopsis/solicitation. 

A list of line-item number(s) and items, quantities, and units of measure (including option(s), if applicable). 

See Attachment 1 Offer For Standard Services/Section B Pricing Sheet 

Description of requirements for the items to be acquired, including documentation supporting any brand name descriptions (see 12.102). 

See Section C of RFP 

Date(s) and place(s) of delivery and acceptance and f.o.b. point (see part 47). 

A list of solicitation provisions that apply to the acquisition (see 12.205(a)). See Section L of RFP  

A list of contract clauses that apply to the acquisition (see 12.205(b)). See Section I of RFP 

The date, time, and place for receipt of offer and point of contact. 

See 1.6 Proposal Due Date of RFP 

Any other additional information required by 5.101(c). 

None
opps.summary:
Background
 This solicitation, numbered 47QACA26R0274, is issued by the Small Business Administration (SBA) for a Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) aimed at providing loan file review services. The SBA's mission includes overseeing the 7(a) and 504 Loan Programs, which necessitates thorough reviews of loans to ensure compliance with established regulations. This acquisition is specifically set aside for small business concerns, emphasizing the agency's commitment to supporting small enterprises in government contracting.

Work Details
 The contractor will provide comprehensive loan file review

opps.description:
The mission of the Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA) is to improve health 
and achieve health equity through access to quality services, a skilled health workforce and 
innovative programs. HRSA assures the availability of quality health care to low income, 
uninsured, isolated, vulnerable, and special needs populations. As the world's leading public 
health agency, HRSA has the responsibility for promoting health and quality of life by 
preventing and controlling disease, injury and disability. HRSA works with States, local public 
health agencies and partners throughout the Nation and the world to accomplish this mission. 
HRSA serves as the national focus for developing and applying disease prevention and control 
methodologies, environmental health strategies, and health promotion and education activities 
designed to improve the health of the people of the United States and throughout the world. 
The contractor shall be capable of providing Software Development, Operations, Maintenance 
(O&M) support services for Custom, Enterprise Applications and Cloud Platform Systems as 
HRSA functions evolve or are incorporated into broader HHS enterprise or operating models, 
without interruption to services or degradation of security posture.

opps.title: Bioinformatics Analysis of Livestock Genomics
opps.agency: FDA Office of Acquisition and Grant Services
opps.description:
CVM possesses significant bioinformatics expertise; however, personnel with the specific skills to perform these tasks are currently allocated to other high-priority agency projects. To meet the urgent timelines for this analysis, CVM requires external, focused support to perform the necessary visual validation of swine variants from an already generated list of candidate variants and the generation of CRISPRme input files for cattle, chicken, and swine. This approach will allow the project to proceed efficiently while enabling CVM's internal experts to provide oversight and ensure the validated variants and CRISPRme input files meet their precise scientific requirements. 

Please see attached RFQ.
opps.summary:
Background
 The Center for Veterinary Medicine (CVM) at the FDA is seeking external support for bioinformatics analysis due to personnel being allocated to other high-priority projects. 

 The goal is to validate genomic variants in CRISPR/Cas9 gene-edited swine samples and generate CRISPRme input files for cattle, chicken, and swine. This will allow CVM's internal experts to oversee the project while ensuring that the scientific requirements are met efficiently.

Work Details
 The contractor will perform the following tasks: 
1) Visual validation of genomic variants in whole genome sequencing

opps.title: Agile Development Services II (ADS2)
opps.description:
Provide skilled information technology labor services to the Coast Guard CG-C5I-Engineering and C5I Service Center Business Operations Division for Agile application development, database maintenance, and custom decision-making tool implementation to improve and modernize technology service delivery to operational Coast Guard units.

Business Governance and Continuous Process Improvement
- Assess strategic landscapes/events impacting Coast Guard C5I service delivery to define, establish, and update long-term strategic objectives.
- Conduct research and analysis for strategic growth planning and execution guidance.
- Engage organizational stakeholders to plan, support and facilitate meetings, determine solutions to stakeholder problems and develop Courses of Action (COA) options to achieve organization goals. 
- Provide guidance and execution support in defining and measuring effectiveness of governance efforts and assets, and in continually improving governance effectiveness over time.
- Develop and support Continual Process Improvement (CPI) initiatives by tracking progress, measuring success, and providing training products to support these efforts (for example: Lean Six Sigma White and Yellow Belt training).
- Support and provide guidance adopting maturity models, including conducting organizational analyses to research, develop, and execute action plans detailing steps and progress adopting maturity models and steps organizational components should take to mature capabilities in alignment with maturity models.
- Recommend, develop, and assist in implementing strategies and action plans that optimize adoption and effective organizational change management (OCM).

Business Performance
- Assist in developing, implementing, and routinely updating performance measurement systems, metrics frameworks, and toolsets that deliver capabilities that monitor, control, visualize, and maintain organizational functionality.
- Perform data Extract-Transform-Load (ETL) procedures

opps.title: INL Guatemala Automated Biometric Identification System
opps.description:
6/12/2026 UPDATE - RESPONSES TO OFFER QUESTIONS HAVE BEEN POSTED IN THE ATTACHMENTS (2 FILES). SOLICITATION EXTENDED TO 3:00PM EST, THURSDAY, JUNE 18, 2026.

6/3/2026 UPDATE - SOLICITATION EXTENDED TO FRIDAY, JUNE 12, 2026, 12:00 PM TO ALLOW FOR RESPONSES TO OFFEROR QUESTIONS AND PROVIDE UPDATED INFORMATION

5/28/2026 UPDATE - SOLICITATION EXTENDED TO THURSDAY, JUNE 4, 2026. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION SHALL BE FORTHCOMING

SOLICITATION: 19INLE26R0085 - INL Guatemala Automated Biometric Identification System (ABIS)

(PCS: 7H20 NAICS: 518210)

BACKGROUND:

The Department of State (DOS) INL Guatemala is supporting the Guatemalan Migration Institute’s modernization effort through the installation and development of a biometrics-capable migration platform.  This effort includes the design and implementation of a new migration control platform with the capacity to instantly share passenger data with U.S. authorities in certain cases.  In order to fully implement this new platform, Guatemalan authorities need to build a traveler biometric database to register and store biometric-based entry records.  This procurement seeks to obtain server capacity to register and store said records.

ADDITIONAL INSTRUCTIONS TO OFFERORS:

Each offeror shall submit a proposal that clearly and concisely describes and defines the contractor’s response to the requirements contained in the solicitation and Statement of Work.  Use of general or vague statements such as “standard procedures will be used” or “good engineering practices” will not satisfy this requirement. Simply repeating or paraphrasing the RFP requirements without substantive, meaningful discussion will not be acceptable.  Unnecessary elaboration or presentation beyond that which is sufficient to present a complete and effective proposal is not desired and may be construed as an indication of the contractor’s lack of understanding of the requirements or cost consciousness.  Elaborate artwork

No proposals, quotes, or responses are requested or required at this time. This announcement is for informational and planning purposes only; specific instructions for submitting proposals will be provided in the forthcoming solicitation.Solicitation Number: 140G0226R0003Notice Type: Synopsis/Pre-Solicitation NoticeProject Title: Science and Technical Support Services (STSSC) for the Mid-Continent Region (MCR) including Central Midwest Region of IL, Water Mission Area (WMA), and National Water Quality Laboratory (NWQL).Current Places of Performance: Colorado, Montana, North Dakota, Michigan, Minnesota, New York, Wisconsin, and other designated locations.Synopsis: The overall objective of the proposed commercial services IDIQ Contract is to provide science and technical support services required by the U.S. Geological Survey's Mid-Continent Region Mission Area.  The United States Geological Survey (USGS) Mid-Continent Region (MCR) including Central Midwest Area of IL, Water Mission Area (WMA), and National Water Quality Laboratory (NWQL) have a continuing requirement for on and off-site non-personal commercial science and technical support services.  Services under this procurement will provide vital support to USGS. The science and technical support services are required in the following categories: Administrative; Program Management; Research/Science; Data and Information Management; Geospatial Applications, Modeling, and Associated Technologies; Technical Project Management; Facilities and Building Management Support Services; Data Entry Services; Accessioning Support Services; LIMS Support Services Warehouse Services; Physical Science Technical Laboratory Services; CoLabs and Contract Lab Data Coordination Services; Quality Assurance and Data Integrity Support Services; Subject Matter Expertise and Method ValidationP

opps.description:
Request for Quote:

The United States Geological Survey (USGS), Mineral Resource Program, has a requirement for Geographic Information System (GIS) Support Services.

Please provide a firm, fixed price quote and qualifications per the attached Statement of Work and Experience and Capabilities requirements.

Evaluation Method: Technical Experience, Past Performance, and Price.

Location: Spokane, WA 99201

Period of Performance:
Base Award: 10/01/2026 09/30/2027
Option Year: 10/01/2027 09/30/2028

Please provide separate pricing for base award and option year.

This requirement is subject to availability of funds.

Please include the following information with your quote:
- Company's UEI number

Your company must be registered and active in System for Award Management (SAM www.sam.gov) prior to award.
US Geological Survey is tax exempt.

This RFQ is set aside for small businesses only.

Questions must be submitted via email ONLY to victor_nuno@ios.doi.gov by June 8, 2026 
Please respond with quote not later than:  June 15, 2026

- Development or maintenance of custom applications. 
- On-site and off-site contractor support and man

opps.title: HR Staffing Support Services
opps.description:
This is a SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE (not a pre-solicitation notice pursuant to FAR Part 5) to determine the capability of potential sources and the availability of commercial sources pursuant to FAR Part 10.

This notice is issued by the USCG HQ Contracts Division 1 (HCA COCO-1) to identify Small Businesses capable of providing HR Staffing Support Services for USCG and Assistant Commandant for Civilian Personnel (PSC-CPM) for a 12-month base period with an anticipated start date of August 2026.

This Sources Sought does not constitute an Invitation for Bid or a Request for Proposals, nor should it be considered as a commitment on the part of the Government. The information received will not obligate the Government in any manner nor will the Government reimburse companies for any costs associated with submittal of this Sources Sought.

All interested parties should provide responses to the contact representative below by email. Please provide your capability statement to include the following information: business size, name and address of company, point of contact along with phone number, and past performance. Past performance information should include recent and relevant contracts for the same or similar items and other references (including contract numbers, points of contact with telephone numbers and other relevant information). Interested parties are asked to furnish this information for at least three (3) contracts, ongoing or completed within the last three (3) years, for like or similar services, held with the USCG, other Government agencies or private industry.

Questions over the telephone will not be taken. Keep in mind, this is a sources sought to help identify if there are any potential offerors and to research the federal market to understand the competition. Interested parties are responsible for monitoring this site to ensure they have the most up-to-date information regarding this research.

Submit Responses to: Ms. Sherylan Spears, sherylan.spears@usc

Combined Synopsis / Solicitation

Contracting Office: USCG, CG 914-2, Kearneysville, WV

Total Small Business Set-Aside

Brand Name Only requirement – No substitutions



This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products or commercial services prepared in accordance with FAR Part 12 and FAR Class Deviation (Number 25-21). This announcement constitutes only solicitation. Offers are being requested and a separate written solicitation will not be issued.



The solicitation number is 52800PR260007470 and is issued as a Request for Quote (RFQ), unless otherwise indicated herein. This RFQ is for HMS TimeControl Licensing Support and Maintenance.



Submit written offers only, oral offers will not be accepted. This combined synopsis/solicitation requires registration with the System for Award Management (SAM) at the time a quote is submitted, excluding outlined in FAR 4.1102(a). Registration information can be found at http://www.sam.gov. UNIQUE ENTITY IDENTIFIER (UEI) NUMBER, FEDERAL TAX IDENTIFICATION NUMBER, AND CAGE CODE MUST BE INCLUDED.



The solicitation document and incorporated provisions and clauses are those in effect through Federal Acquisition Circular 2025-03. The complete text of any of the provisions and clauses may be accessed in full at http://www.acquisition.gov/browse/index/far.



The procurement is a Firm Fixed Price and the is associated North American Industrial Classification System (NAICS) code is 541519 - Other Computer Related Services. The size standard for this NAICS is $34M. Product Service Code is: 7A21 - IT and Telecom—Business Application Software (Perpetual License Software).



The description of the requirement is required on behalf of United States Coast Guard (USCG). The objective is to procure HMS TimeControl Licensing Support and Maintenance to be renewed for the period of 6 July 2026 – 5 July 2027.

opps.title: NHLBI Innovation and Commercialization Ecosystem: Product Development Support and Program Evaluation
opps.description:
The National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NHLBI), Innovation and Commercialization Office (I&C), is conducting market research to identify qualified small business sources capable of providing innovation and commercialization ecosystem support services. The anticipated requirement includes program evaluation and analytics, partner engagement and commercialization support, operation and enhancement of an AI-enabled partnering platform, program coordination and logistics, entrepreneurial education and mentoring, and support for Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) initiatives.

The Government is seeking capability statements from small business concerns capable of performing the activities described in the attached draft Statement of Work. Responses will be used for acquisition planning purposes, including assessing the availability of qualified small business sources and determining an appropriate acquisition strategy.

(See additional details in the attached Sources Sought Notice)
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NHLBI), part of the Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS), is seeking to identify qualified small business sources for providing innovation and commercialization ecosystem support services. The goal is to enhance the translation of scientific discoveries into diagnostics, devices, and therapeutics by supporting the small business innovation ecosystem, particularly through Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs.

The NHLBI's Innovation and Commercialization Office (I&C)

opps.title: Thales FY26
opps.description: USCIS has a requirement to obtain the software license renewal for Thales Device HSM (Hardware Security Module) as a Service which is a key component of the overall device certificate automation project. An HSM is a specialized physical device designed to securely store and manage digital keys, cryptographic operations, and sensitive information. They provide a high level of protection for cryptographic processes and serve as a secure foundation for various security applications. Additionally, they offer a range of features including key generation, storage and protection, cryptographic operations (such as encryption and decryption), secure key exchange, and digital signatures. By employing tamper-resistant hardware and robust security mechanisms, HSMs help safeguard sensitive data and cryptographic keys from unauthorized access and tampering attempts. They also support compliance with regulatory standards and provide a trusted platform for building secure systems.

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:
Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi
Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

opps.title: J6 Program Management Office Support Services
opps.description: USSOCOM has a requirement for Program Management Office Support Services (PMOSS) in support of USSOCOM J6.  This requirement provides management, planning, and financial support that helps USSOCOM oversee programs, resources, budgets, contracts, and organizational initiatives. The contractor assists government leadership with planning future requirements, tracking performance, managing documentation, monitoring contractor services, coordinating working groups and meetings, analyzing data, supporting budget execution, and helping ensure programs are carried out efficiently and in accordance with government policies and objectives. The contract serves as a support function that enables informed decision-making, effective resource management, and successful execution of USSOCOM priorities.
opps.summary:
Background
 USSOCOM, formed in 1987, is a unified combatant command of the Department of Defense (DoD) tasked with developing and employing fully capable special operations forces. Its mission includes conducting global special operations to support U.S. policies and objectives against various actors. USSOCOM oversees training and equipping all DoD Special Operations Forces (SOF) for missions worldwide.

opps.title: Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR)
opps.description:
***5/28/26 - updated set-aside, Solicitation Release Date, and Period of Performanace***

The U.S. Citizenship & Immigration Services (USCIS) has a requirement for Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR), consisting of teams to provide development, security, operations (DevSecOps), data processing and analytic services to support USCIS, Information Technology (IT) development delivery services. These services primarily provide identification of Fraud, National Security and Public Safety concerns. The teams focus will be on performing development, security, and operational support activities. The team will also perform, enhancements and refinements of algorithms as well as standardizing large-scale, complex, internal and external applications. 

All applications in the portfolio employ a baseline set of DevSecOps development postures along with USCIS required development gates, safeguards and requirements. They can be categorized into several overarching categories: Case Management, Investigation, Data Storage / Operations, Screening and Vetting, Immigration Operations, Immigration Law Enforcement, and Intelligence Operations.

*Currently performing market research*
win_reasons: Partial cloud/modernization match — Clarity24 JV has NOAA cloud delivery experience
Small business set-aside
OUT OF SCOPE: ship — not in BLN24 delivery lanes
⚠️ Scope gap: DevSecOps — BLN24 has no confirmed prime delivery in these lanes

tasks.note: BLN24 prime — Census SRQA, MAF/TIGER, EDL all heavy data engineering
Adjacent (2/4) — Census SRQA used agile + DevSecOps principles; IRS Forms Mod used CI/CD pipelines. Not standalone DevSecOps prime but confirmed delivery within broader contracts.
BLN24 gap — no confirmed prime cybersecurity delivery; would need dedicated cyber partner

capability_matrix.fit_reason: Majority of task areas require teaming — may not be best lead

qualifying.q: Do we have the capabilities needed?

qualifying.answer: Forecast — early awareness, shape before RFP
$50050.0M — Tier 2+ (BLN24 has won at this range)

rex_analysis.what_they_need:
***5/28/26 - updated set-aside, Solicitation Release Date, and Period of Performanace***

The U.S. Citizenship & Immigration Services (USCIS) has a requirement for Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR), consisting of teams to provide development, security, operations (DevSecOps), data processing and analytic services to support USCIS, Information Technology (IT) development delivery services.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 can cover: Data Engineering & ETL. Gap areas requiring teaming: Cybersecurity / FISMA / Zero Trust. Evaluate whether the BLN24 scope is large enough to prime.
rex_analysis.watch_notes: Review the full solicitation at source. Set-aside: SBA. Notice type: FORECAST.

opps.title: PR16018614 - DoS-Sources Sought
opps.description:
The Department of State’s Office to Monitor and Combat Trafficking in Persons (TIP Office) leads the United States’ global engagement in the fight against human trafficking, partnering with foreign governments and civil society to develop and implement effective anti-trafficking strategies. The TIP Office works to fulfill the mandates of combating human trafficking globally as outlined in the Trafficking Victims Protection Act of 2000, as amended (TVPA), and has responsibility for bilateral and multilateral diplomacy, targeted foreign assistance, interagency coordination, and public engagement on trafficking in persons.



One of the roles of the TIP Office is to provide support for the U.S. government’s interagency coordinating bodies, the President’s Interagency Task Force to Monitor and Combat Trafficking in Persons (PITF) and the Senior Policy Operating Group (SPOG), both established under the TVPA.  In this role, the TIP Office supports the United States Advisory Council on Human Trafficking (Advisory Council), which was established by the Survivors of Human Trafficking Empowerment Act within the Justice for Victims of Trafficking Act enacted on May 22, 2015.  The Advisory Council is an independent, nongovernmental advisory body that advises and makes recommendations on federal anti-trafficking policies to the PITF.  The Council is appointed by the President for two-year terms and may serve up to two terms.

opps.title: Enterprise Gateway and Integration Services (EGIS) III
opps.description:
USCIS has a requirement for Enterprise Gateway and Integration Services (EGIS) III. Historically, EGIS has served as the enterprise integration backbone connecting USCIS internal systems with Department of Homeland Security (DHS), interagency, and external partners. While this core mission remains critical, EGIS will also expand this role supporting national security, immigration system integrity and advanced adjudication operations with key mission including: National security vetting and evolving immigration policies; screening immigration benefit applicants; protecting immigration benefits and work authorization integrity; advanced data integration, analytics, AI-enabled anomaly detection, and decision support; agility, rapid delivery, operational excellence, transparency, and reduced duplication; fraud detection, risk scoring, and investigative data workflows. 
As of 3/3/2026 we are in the market research/ acquisition planning phase of this acquisition, as such, some of the APFS fields may change over the coming months. This record will continually be updated once changes to the strategy are known.

opps.title: Enterprise Cloud Customer Relationship Management Platform (ECCRMP) Services
opps.description:
The subject RFQ is issued to Small Business vendors for Enterprise Cloud Customer Relationship Management Platform (ECCRMP) Services. The period of performance (PoP) for the task order for a seven (7) month transition in/base period, seven (7) month option period 1, and four (4) twelve (12) month option periods.

CBP contemplates the issuance of a Firm Fixed Price (FFP) task order (TO) with in-scope Surge Support addressed through optional Labor-Hour (LH) CLIN resulting from this solicitation.  Please note that in addition to the terms and conditions of the GSA MAS IDIQ, the order will be subject to the terms and conditions in Attachment 5, Additional Provisions, Clauses, and Terms and Conditions. The basis for award will be to arrive at a best value decision, evaluating both the technical response and price quote.

For additional information on quote submission requirements, the quote evaluation process, including the factors and evaluation criteria, and quote documentation, please refer to the "Instructions to Quoters" document attached herein.

Questions/Clarifications: Any questions or clarification requests regarding any aspect of this RFQ shall be submitted by 1pm ET, Wednesday, May 06, 2026 via GSA MAS.

Quotes: Quote responses are to be submitted NLT 1pm ET, Tuesday, May 26, 2026 via GSA MAS.

Please note, funding is currently not available, and award is contingent upon funding availability.

opps.title: National Centers for Environmental Information (NCEI) Midwestern Regional Weather Records Center (MRWRC)
opps.description:
6/9/2026 - Amendment 0001 attachments and Responses to RFP Questions and Comments are posted. Proposal due date extended to June 26, 2026.

1. Introduction and Background

The mission of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s (NOAA) National Environmental Satellite, Data, and Information Service (NESDIS) is to provide and ensure timely access to global environmental data from satellites and other sources to promote, protect, and enhance the Nation's economy, security, environment, and quality of life. NESDIS acquires and manages the Nation's operational environmental satellites, provides data and information services to numerous end users, and conducts related research.

Since 1990, NCEI’s RCS program has traditionally supported Regional Weather Records Centers (RWRCs) established at Public Universities within six geographic regions. For more than forty years, Regional Centers have produced and delivered environmental data, products, information, and services – hereafter collectively termed “products and services” – and captured user needs. By transforming, tailoring, and augmenting NCEI’s products and services, the RWRCs provide a bridge between NCEI and regional user communities, including the private sector. The RWRCs support NOAA’s mission to advance understanding of long-term weather variability and impacts through user engagement and the translation of data into usable products and services for decision makers.

2. Scope of Work

A. Geographic Scope

This contract is for the Midwestern Regional Weather Records Center (MRWRC), which is composed of KY, IN, MI, IL, WI, MO, IA, MN, and OH.

The contractor providing the MRWRC capability to a region shall be physically located within the region and must serve all states, territories, and U.S.-affiliated islands in the region. An RWRC with unique capabilities may work with other RWRCs to meet user needs beyond its immediate region.

B. Program Sco

opps.title: National Centers for Environmental Information (NCEI) Southern Regional Weather Records Center (SRWRC)
opps.description:
6/9/2026 - Amendment 0001 attachments and Responses to RFP Questions and Comments are posted. Proposal due date extended to June 26, 2026.

1. Introduction and Background

The mission of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s (NOAA) National Environmental Satellite, Data, and Information Service (NESDIS) is to provide and ensure timely access to global environmental data from satellites and other sources to promote, protect, and enhance the Nation's economy, security, environment, and quality of life. NESDIS acquires and manages the Nation's operational environmental satellites, provides data and information services to numerous end users, and conducts related research.

Since 1990, NCEI’s RCS program has traditionally supported Regional Weather Records Centers (RWRCs) established at Public Universities within six geographic regions. For more than forty years, Regional Centers have produced and delivered environmental data, products, information, and services – hereafter collectively termed “products and services” – and captured user needs. By transforming, tailoring, and augmenting NCEI’s products and services, the RWRCs provide a bridge between NCEI and regional user communities, including the private sector. The RWRCs support NOAA’s mission to advance understanding of long-term weather variability and impacts through user engagement and the translation of data into usable products and services for decision makers.

2. Scope of Work

A. Geographic Scope

This contract is for the Southern Regional Weather Records Center (SRWRC), which is composed of TN, MS, LA, AR, OK, and TX.

The contractor providing the SRWRC capability to a region shall be physically located within the region and must serve all states, territories, and U.S.-affiliated islands in the region. An RWRC with unique capabilities may work with other RWRCs to meet user needs beyond its immediate region.

B. Program Scope

The SRWRC

opps.title: Google Workspace Enterprise Plus
opps.description:
This procurement, supporting the Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO), is for the use of Google Workspace Enterprise Plus Apps to support CISAâ€™s IT Operations performance functions. The Enterprise Plus apps are a cloud-based browser with built-in controls that provide encryption and verification which enables work from anywhere and eliminates the need for VPNs. The apps have the ability to operate on a global scale while protecting information from phishing, malware, ransomware, and supply chain attacks. And the apps have secure endpoints that do not require patching and strong account takeover protections.

The software will be used to analyze CISA datasets to enhance leadership awareness of trends and organizational performance. The software procured must be capable of conducting real- time analysis; be relatively easy to use; allow data to flow easily into and out of the software; have forecasting capabilities that can predict values based on trends in the dataset; utilize user- friendly dashboards to format visualizations; and support professional reporting formats and data visualizations suitable for executive-level presentations.

 The contractor shall provide the CISA Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) with the following: 
1. Google Workspace Enterprise Plus; New â€“ Quantity: 1,300, 
2. Google Workspace Enterprise Plus; Archived â€“ Quantity: 1,300, 
3. G Suite Support Base Fee; - Quantity: 1, 
4. G Suite Support Variable Fee; - Quantity: 1,
5. Cloud Platform â€“ Support G Suite; Quantity: 50,000

opps.agency: USGS Office of Acquisitions and Grants
opps.description: This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for a commercial product/service prepared in accordance with the format in Subpart 12.6, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This Request for Quotation (RFQ) constitutes the only solicitation; quotations are being requested and a written solicitation is being issued, which can be found on www.sam.gov. The solicitation number is 140G0126Q0175 and is issued as an RFQ. The US Geological Survey (USGS) has a requirement for the operation of a NADP/NTN wet atmospheric monitoring station. Operation requires weekly site visits to the site to collect process and ship the precipitation sample, collect, process and transmit associated data with the site including the precipitation gage record and perform routine instrument checks, maintenance and repairs as needed.All vendors who can meet this requirement and are registered in the System for Award Management are invited to submit electronic quotes for this posted RFQ. All questions shall be submitted via email to the Contracting Officer, Brian Baker, at bfbaker@usgs.gov no later than 1:00 PM EST, Friday, June 12, 2026.Quotation packages are due via email to the Contracting Officer, Brian Baker, at bfbaker@usgs.gov no later than 1:00 PM EST, Friday, June 19, 2026.
opps.summary:

 The US Geological Survey (USGS) is seeking to operate a NADP/NTN wet atmospheric monitoring station as part of its mission to monitor environmental conditions. Work Details

- Collect, process, and transmit associated data from the site, including maintaining records of precipi

Leadership and team development services 

I am requesting a firm fixed price quote.

This will be evaluated based on the below four factors:

- Corporate experience and experience of key personnel and/or subcontractors. Candidate(s) must have at least three (3) years of experience of relevant work as related to the SOW. 
- Capability, including technical capability and capacity to perform in the required time.
-Past performance 
- Price

Period of performance: approximately 09/15/2026 to 09/14/2027 with specific timelines for two-day strategy sessions (est. Sept. '26 and March '27), that need to be met.

USGS is tax exempt. 

Vendor should be current and active in System for Award Management (SAM) or UEI (Unique Entity ID) to submit a quote

opps.title: DTMO Travel Operations Management Support Services (TOMSS)
opps.agency: Office of the Secretary of Defense
opps.description:
The Defense Travel Management Office (DTMO) requires enterprise wide operational
management and support for the Integrated Lodging Programs that will enable
organizational leadership to make informed, fact-based decisions that positively impact the daily
execution of mission requirements and the development of longer-term strategies. The Travel
Programs Branch's strategic objective is to adopt lodging industry best practices to
leverage strategic sourcing opportunities; and to fully employ the strategic sourcing management
philosophy to better meet DoD's requirements while generating savings for the Department of
Defense.  This will be a Firm-Fixed-Price contract with Time-and-Material CLINS for Travel.
opps.summary:
Background
 The Defense Travel Management Office (DTMO), part of the Defense Human Resources Activity (DHRA), is seeking enterprise-wide operational management and support for the Integrated Lodging Programs. Established to centralize commercial travel within the Department of War (DoW), DTMO aims to leverage strategic sourcing opportunities and adopt industry best practices to enhance lodging services for DoW travelers. 

This contract is a follow-on requirement to an existing contract, emphasizing the need for informed decision-making in daily mission execution and long-term strategy develop

opps.title: DOC NIST - Gaithersburg, MD Data Center Co-Location - MRAS
opps.agency: National Institute of Standards and Technology
opps.description:
On behalf of Department of Commerce (DOC), GSA is conducting market research.

DO NOT submit your response in eBuy. Your response will not be included in the report that is provided to the customer. NOTE: If the link does not work, try clearing your browser history (cache) and then copying and pasting the link into a new browser. To respond to this RFI, you MUST copy and paste the below link into a new browser.

Only responses submitted through the linked RFI will be accepted. Nothing else will be evaluated or recorded (this includes selecting 'interested' within eBuy).


Link to RFI: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_e2QwibOi1b6S1NQ


Additional Information:
Due to the structure of contracts in GSA eBuy, you may receive multiple Sources Sought or RFI notifications for the same requirement if it falls under multiple of your company's awarded SINs. However, please note that you are only required to respond to the RFI once.

Be sure to use the URL provided above to submit your response.


Any questions regarding this RFI will be responded to by email only.  Send your inquiry to: rfi@research.gsa.gov and be sure to include the RFQ number.
opps.summary:
Background
 The Department of Commerce (DOC) is seeking market research on data center colocation services through the General Services Administration (GSA). The goal is to gather information for budget planning related to future scientific computing equipment procurement and hosting costs. 

The anticipated equipment includes a High Performance Computing (HPC) system with significant power and cooling demands, which will be hosted within a 50-mile radius of the NIST Gaithersburg, MD campus.

Work Details
 The Computer Facility Services Team (CFST) is conducting market research to obtain Rough

OUT OF SCOPE: equipment procurement — not in BLN24 delivery lanes
